sessions.t: "Self-Driving" SOC: Why AI Needs a Human Pilot
sessions.desc:
The industry is buzzing about the “Autonomous SOC”, but the “AI-only” narrative often falls short of reality. In this session, Deepwatch dissects the true division of labor in a modern defense strategy: where AI shines (massive data synthesis, query generation) and where humans are non-negotiable (nuanced decision-making, critical risk acceptance). Learn why the future of SecOps isn't about replacing analysts and how Deepwatch MDR combines its Agentic AI Ecosystem with elite human experts to deliver outcomes that algorithms alone cannot match.



By attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow up for this event only.
sessions.q:
"self-driving" soc: why ai needs a human pilot the industry is buzzing about the “autonomous soc”, but the “ai-only” narrative often falls short of reality. in this session, deepwatch dissects the true division of labor in a modern defense strategy: where ai shines (massive data synthesis, query generation) and where humans are non-negotiable (nuanced decision-making, critical risk acceptance). learn why the future of secops isn't about replacing analysts and how deepwatch mdr combines its agentic ai ecosystem with elite human experts to deliver outcomes that algorithms alone cannot match.



by attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow up for this event only. anand ramanathan deepwatch, inc. sponsored sponsor sponsor_disclosure

sessions.t: [U.S. Military & Veterans]  Leading AI & Cloud from Uniform to Corporate
sessions.desc: Join veteran leaders from Google Cloud and Google Public Sector—including CEO Karen Dahut (Navy Veteran), CTO Will Grannis (Army Veteran), and Managing Director Earl Matthews (Air Force Veteran)—for a dynamic panel exploring the transition from military service to tech leadership. This session, tailored for active service members and veterans, bridges mission-first experience with market demands. The discussion will cover three critical areas: translating military discipline into corporate strategy, diving into technical scaling and compliance challenges, and providing a career roadmap for senior leaders looking to manage large-scale digital transformation and AI initiatives
sessions.q: [u.s. military & veterans]  leading ai & cloud from uniform to corporate join veteran leaders from google cloud and google public sector—including ceo karen dahut (navy veteran), cto will grannis (army veteran), and managing director earl matthews (air force veteran)—for a dynamic panel exploring the transition from military service to tech leadership. this session, tailored for active service members and veterans, bridges mission-first experience with market demands. the discussion will cover three critical areas: translating military discipline into corporate strategy, diving into technical scaling and compliance challenges, and providing a career roadmap for senior leaders looking to manage large-scale digital transformation and ai initiatives karen dahut google public sector will grannis google cloud earl matthews google cloud christian michael google

sessions.t: 5 embedding decisions every cloud developer needs to know
sessions.desc: Latency, accuracy, and retrieval costs are make-or-break metrics for AI applications on Google Cloud. In this deep dive, Pete Johnson, MongoDB’s AI field CTO, will explore the “Big 5” configuration decisions that define the success of retrieval-augmented generation: chunking, similarity functions, dimensionality, quantization, and reranking. Learn how Voyage AI’s cutting-edge models and MongoDB Atlas let you optimize these parameters while hosting data in any Google Cloud data center. You’ll gain the technical know-how to fine-tune your embedding strategy for maximum efficiency. By attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow-up for this event only.

sessions.t: A deep dive into Gemini Enterprise governance, security, and compliance
sessions.desc: As enterprises graduate from initial AI pilots to full-scale production, the focus shifts from “What can generative AI do?” to “How do we govern and secure it at scale?” Join this technical deep dive that unpacks the multilayered security architecture of Gemini Enterprise, providing a blueprint for deploying, orchestrating, and governing complex agent ecosystems. We’ll go beyond theory into the practical implementation of defense-in-depth strategies, dissecting the three critical AI security layers – content, network, and identity – and demonstrating how to implement centralized governance. You’ll leave with a comprehensive enterprise-readiness checklist, equipping you with the specific controls needed to launch secure, compliant AI agents today.

sessions.t: A peek into the future: How agents transform SRE
sessions.desc: Site reliability engineering (SRE) teams can no longer scale linearly to manage the complexities of modern microservices. Agents are evolving from simple chatbots into an autonomous “AI army” capable of managing the entire service life cycle. This session provides a technical playbook for deploying a swarm of specialized agents – from architect and rollout agents to troubleshooting and maintenance agents. Learn how Google and PayPal use an SRE orchestrator agent to correlate signals and reduce blast radius. Join this session and leave with a blueprint for transitioning from human-in-the-loop oversight to fully autonomous, self-healing operations.

sessions.t: Accelerate data science and analytics with GPUs
sessions.desc: Data teams rely on pandas and scikit-learn for their simplicity, but performance degrades as datasets grow. This session reveals how to break those barriers without abandoning the tools you know. We will show you how to use GPUs in Colab Enterprise to accelerate standard Python workflows. Using NVIDIA libraries like cuDF and cuML, we will demonstrate how to drop-in replace libraries to achieve massive speedups. Join us for a live demo where we flip the switch to GPUs with zero code changes.

sessions.t: Accelerate domain AI agents on Google Cloud Vertex AI with NVIDIA NeMo and NVIDIA Nemotron
sessions.desc:
Join NVIDIA experts to explore how the NVIDIA NeMo framework and Nemotron models integrate with Google Cloud Vertex AI to deliver a frontier-scale  platform for building and deploying domain-specialized AI agents. We'll cover the technology stack, showing how NVIDIA’s models,  libraries, and Blackwell GPUs, combined with Vertex AI made for efficient training, fine-tuning, RL, and scalable inference

The session will feature a real-world case study from leading cybersecurity provider CrowdStrike, illustrating how they use this suite of solutions on Vertex with NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs to fine-tune LLMs for cybersecurity, accelerating threat detection and response.

We’ll close by offering learnings and best practices to customize Nemotron and other open models from the Vertex AI Model Garden.

accelerate domain ai agents on google cloud vertex ai with nvidia nemo and nvidia nemotron join nvidia experts to explore how the nvidia nemo framework and nemotron models integrate with google cloud vertex ai to deliver a frontier-scale  platform for building and deploying domain-specialized ai agents. we'll cover the technology stack, showing how nvidia’s models,  libraries, and blackwell gpus, combined with vertex ai made for efficient training, fine-tuning, rl, and scalable inference

sessions.t: Accelerate Robot Learning With NVIDIA Isaac Lab and Newton
sessions.desc: In this hands-on lab, you will explore how to dramatically enhance synthetic training data for Physical AI using NVIDIA’s Cosmos and Isaac Sim, all powered by Google Cloud G4 (RTX Pro 6000) instances. You will step through a complete, simulation-focused data-generation pipeline, starting by exploring a baseline environment in Isaac Sim to capture an initial dataset of robotic demonstrations. Next, you will enrich this baseline data using Cosmos, applying generative AI to create photorealistic, physically consistent variations with diverse lighting, textures, and object placements based on simple text prompts. Finally, you will conduct a side-by-side comparison of the datasets and policy rollouts, evaluating the visual diversity and robustness of models trained purely on standard simulation data versus those trained on the Cosmos-generated dataset to see firsthand how generative modeling on Google Cloud unlocks significant performance gains.
sessions.q: accelerate robot learning with nvidia isaac lab and newton in this hands-on lab, you will explore how to dramatically enhance synthetic training data for physical ai using nvidia’s cosmos and isaac sim, all powered by google cloud g4 (rtx pro 6000) instances. you will step through a complete, simulation-focused data-generation pipeline, starting by exploring a baseline environment in isaac sim to capture an initial dataset of robotic demonstrations. next, you will enrich this baseline data using cosmos, applying generative ai to create photorealistic, physically consistent variations with diverse lighting, textures, and object placements based on simple text prompts. finally, you will conduct a side-by-side comparison of the datasets and policy rollouts, evaluating the visual diversity and robustness of models trained purely on standard simulation data versus those trained on the cosmos-generated dataset to see firsthand how generative modeling on google cloud unlocks significant performance gains. rishabh chadha nvidia aiden chang nvidia chuck freeman us nvidia adrian graham nvidia

Learn how Google Cloud VMware Engine provides a low-friction solution for fast, easy migration and modernization. VMware Engine transforms your VMware estate with speed and confidence, enabling you to move existing systems without re-architecting apps and to continue using familiar tools and skill sets. Join us for exciting new announcements and to learn how to rapidly accelerate your cloud-to-AI transformation.

sessions.t: Accelerated modernization: Navigating the journey from SQL Server to PostgreSQL
sessions.desc: Legacy SQL Server environments often anchor organizations to high licensing costs and rigid infrastructure, stifling the agility needed for modern growth. This session explores the technical and strategic roadmap for escaping those constraints by migrating to AlloyDB. Through the lens of Extric (Towbook), you will see how they modernized their application and transitioned away from restrictive legacy systems. By adopting AlloyDB’s built-in AI, Extric catalyzed innovation and slashed TCO, providing a proven blueprint for enhancing performance.
sessions.q: accelerated modernization: navigating the journey from sql server to postgresql legacy sql server environments often anchor organizations to high licensing costs and rigid infrastructure, stifling the agility needed for modern growth. this session explores the technical and strategic roadmap for escaping those constraints by migrating to alloydb. through the lens of extric (towbook), you will see how they modernized their application and transitioned away from restrictive legacy systems. by adopting alloydb’s built-in ai, extric catalyzed innovation and slashed tco, providing a proven blueprint for enhancing performance. apoorv shrivastava google cloud dan smith towbook

sessions.t: Accelerating alpha: Citadel's hybrid HPC and AI strategy
sessions.desc: In the high-stakes world of quantitative finance, faster time to insight is the ultimate competitive advantage. Join leaders from Citadel and Google Cloud to explore how top financial services firms are redefining speed using large-scale Tensor Processing Units (TPUs). We’ll dissect Citadel’s “agentic engineering” approach to automating developer workflows and the architectural realities of evolving from on-premises to a hybrid, cloud-first high performance computing (HPC) environment. Discover strategies to balance extreme performance with agility to accelerate your own research infrastructure.

sessions.t: Achieve multicloud security at scale with Google Unified Security
sessions.desc: The divide between proactive risk management and reactive threat response has become a critical vulnerability. For chief information security officers today, the challenge is no longer just seeing the threat – it’s understanding its business context fast enough to matter. In this breakout, we’ll share how Google is redefining security operations by fusing world-class threat detection and response with deep environmental context. You’ll learn how we’re breaking down silos by integrating rich risk insights from leading partners directly into the security operations center workflow, empowering teams to prioritize incidents based on real-world business risk rather than just technical severity.

sessions.t: Achieve state-of-the-art inference: High performance on TPUs and GPUs with llm-d
sessions.desc: Proprietary stacks and generic open source solutions often lack deep hardware integration. Break free from proprietary constraints with llm-d, an open source stack that delivers state-of-the-art performance across Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) and NVIDIA GPUs. This session dives deep into how to architect disaggregated serving and automatic key-value cache storage tiering on Ironwood (TPU7x). Learn to implement routing optimized for service-level objectives and build a portable, high-performance inference fleet that scales automatically based on real-time server conditions. Leave with a reference architecture for hardware-optimized LLM serving.
sessions.q: achieve state-of-the-art inference: high performance on tpus and gpus with llm-d proprietary stacks and generic open source solutions often lack deep hardware integration. break free from proprietary constraints with llm-d, an open source stack that delivers state-of-the-art performance across tensor processing units (tpus) and nvidia gpus. this session dives deep into how to architect disaggregated serving and automatic key-value cache storage tiering on ironwood (tpu7x). learn to implement routing optimized for service-level objectives and build a portable, high-performance inference fleet that scales automatically based on real-time server conditions. leave with a reference architecture for hardware-optimized llm serving. sean horgan google cloud greg pereira redhat alex zakonov google cloud

sessions.t: After software: Anthropic's vision for the next era of enterprise AI
sessions.desc:
Enterprise software will change more in the next two years than it has in the last twenty. The model is inverting: top-down planning, where organizations define and prioritize what gets built, is giving way to bottom-up execution, where AI agents help teams solve problems as fast as they find them. Anthropic shares what we're seeing at the frontier. Teams are already deploying agents on Vertex AI that define, build, and iterate autonomously. We'll ground the vision in real examples and practical frameworks for readiness.

sessions.t: Agent context engineering for production
sessions.desc: Homegrown sessions and state management can get you started, and local development environments can rely on filesystems and Git. But for scalable, maintainable deployed agents, you need more. Every LLM turn is a compiled view across the current state, ongoing session context, memory, various artifacts, tool responses, examples, and instructions. Join this session to discover how to go from “it works” to “it works great at scale” with context engineering best practices, architectures, and services.
sessions.q: agent context engineering for production homegrown sessions and state management can get you started, and local development environments can rely on filesystems and git. but for scalable, maintainable deployed agents, you need more. every llm turn is a compiled view across the current state, ongoing session context, memory, various artifacts, tool responses, examples, and instructions. join this session to discover how to go from “it works” to “it works great at scale” with context engineering best practices, architectures, and services. jeff dixon at&t george lee google cloud kimberly milam google cloud preethi prabhakar at&t

sessions.t: Agent identity and certificate management
sessions.desc: The shift to agent-driven innovation requires rethinking identity and access management. Join this interactive discussion to explore the challenges and solutions for unifying agent, human, and nonhuman identities. Exchange ideas with peers on moving away from long-lived keys, adopting open standards like SPIFFE, and granting verifiable cryptographic identities to AI agents. Share your blueprints and questions on securing the transition to autonomous AI operations.

sessions.t: Agent Identity as the backbone for secure AI innovation
sessions.desc: The rise of agents demands a fundamental shift in how we define identity. In this session, we’ll share how Google Cloud is unifying agent, human, and nonhuman identity into a standards-based platform designed for a new AI landscape. We’ll also explore how to move beyond the security risks of long-lived, impersonable keys to a future built on open standards like Secure Production Identity Framework for Everyone (SPIFFE). Learn how we’re elevating agents to first-class citizens in the identity and access management world, giving them verifiable, cryptographic identities that allow them to operate with autonomy, security, and full governance. Get the blueprint for securing the transition from human-driven tasks to agent-driven innovation.
sessions.q: agent identity as the backbone for secure ai innovation the rise of agents demands a fundamental shift in how we define identity. in this session, we’ll share how google cloud is unifying agent, human, and nonhuman identity into a standards-based platform designed for a new ai landscape. we’ll also explore how to move beyond the security risks of long-lived, impersonable keys to a future built on open standards like secure production identity framework for everyone (spiffe). learn how we’re elevating agents to first-class citizens in the identity and access management world, giving them verifiable, cryptographic identities that allow them to operate with autonomy, security, and full governance. get the blueprint for securing the transition from human-driven tasks to agent-driven innovation. shaun liu google cloud ravi shah google cloud

sessions.t: Agent security at scale: Protect against the OWASP Top 10 application risks
sessions.desc: Agentic applications can include multiple agents, tools, and model calls, creating a broad and complex attack surface. As developers move into production with these applications, security teams need to make sure they are thoroughly secured from critical threats identified by the Open Worldwide Application Security Project (OWASP), including tool misuse and exploitation, prompt injection, and code hallucination, among others. In this session, we will help you understand new risks associated with agents, as well as key mitigation strategies and ways to implement those strategies using Google Cloud platform controls and security capabilities.
sessions.q: agent security at scale: protect against the owasp top 10 application risks agentic applications can include multiple agents, tools, and model calls, creating a broad and complex attack surface. as developers move into production with these applications, security teams need to make sure they are thoroughly secured from critical threats identified by the open worldwide application security project (owasp), including tool misuse and exploitation, prompt injection, and code hallucination, among others. in this session, we will help you understand new risks associated with agents, as well as key mitigation strategies and ways to implement those strategies using google cloud platform controls and security capabilities. shelly hershkovitz google cloud francois-xavier jeannet geotab aniket patankar google cloud devesh yamparala snap

sessions.t: Agentic AI: social commerce, webtoons, and the future of personalized experiences
sessions.desc:
The era of the ""single prompt"" is over. To solve complex, real-world problems—from automating creator engagement to localizing artistic webcomics—industry leaders are moving toward sophisticated agent architectures. Join Stan and Toonsutra for an inside look at how they orchestrated specialized AI agents to automate creativity, commerce, and personalization at scale on Google Cloud.

Presenting Companies
Stan | The Creator Economy Agent: Discover how Stan (getstan.app) is revolutionizing how creators manage their businesses. Learn how they deployed agentic workflows to automate high-intent interactions, turning a creator's link-in-bio into a proactive sales engine. We’ll break down the architecture behind their ""AI Assistant"" agents that handle everything from lead qualification to personalized product recommendations, allowing creators to scale their impact without increasing their workload.
Toonsutra | The Multimodal Localization Pipeline: India’s #1 webtoon platform reveals their four-agent automated engine for vision-based text extraction and ""chapter-aware"" character profiling, alongside in-context image editing—replacing dialogue inside speech bubbles while perfectly preserving original typography, alignment, and character voice across Indian languages.
sessions.q:
agentic ai: social commerce, webtoons, and the future of personalized experiences the era of the ""single prompt"" is over. to solve complex, real-world problems—from automating creator engagement to localizing artistic webcomics—industry leaders are moving toward sophisticated agent architectures. join stan and toonsutra for an inside look at how they orchestrated specialized ai agents to automate creativity, commerce, and personalization at scale on google cloud.

sessions.t: Agentic commerce: The new automation curve reshaping markets
sessions.desc:
For decades, commerce was optimized for humans clicking, scrolling, and comparing. Now AI agents are reshaping discovery, decisions, and transactions, with the potential to influence $3-5 trillion in B2C sales by 2030. This isn’t fully autonomous shopping; it’s consumer-controlled delegation along a new automation curve. In this era of agentic commerce, power shifts to customers and the agents they deploy. Learn how leaders can prepare their organizations and architectures for growth and resilience in this third commerce revolution.

sessions.t: Agentic commerce: Transform the shopping experience with Google agents and open standards
sessions.desc: Imagine an AI agent that can find the perfect product, answer fit questions, verify inventory in real time, and securely complete a purchase – all within a single chat. This session shows how to make that vision a reality and set your business up for success on your own site, AI Mode in Google Search, Gemini, and beyond. Learn to build state-of-the-art agents with product discovery, equipped with user-event optimized recommendations and deep user context. Dive into how to connect your agents to store catalogs, payment processors, and any conversational UI via the Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP). Discover how to establish irrefutable proof of user authorization using the Agent Payments Protocol (AP2). Join us to learn how these protocols – built on the foundations of Model Context Protocol (MCP) and the Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol – are creating a seamless, secure, and open ecosystem for the next generation of online and in-store retail and commerce.
sessions.q: agentic commerce: transform the shopping experience with google agents and open standards imagine an ai agent that can find the perfect product, answer fit questions, verify inventory in real time, and securely complete a purchase – all within a single chat. this session shows how to make that vision a reality and set your business up for success on your own site, ai mode in google search, gemini, and beyond. learn to build state-of-the-art agents with product discovery, equipped with user-event optimized recommendations and deep user context. dive into how to connect your agents to store catalogs, payment processors, and any conversational ui via the universal commerce protocol (ucp). discover how to establish irrefutable proof of user authorization using the agent payments protocol (ap2). join us to learn how these protocols – built on the foundations of model context protocol (mcp) and the agent2agent (a2a) protocol – are creating a seamless, secure, and open ecosystem for the next generation of online and in-store retail and commerce. jesse carah google cloud hiren patel wayfair brian peters paypal

sessions.t: Agentic FinOps for BigQuery cost optimization
sessions.desc: This talk draws from multi-year BigQuery optimization engagements with a leading hyperscaler enterprises. While traditional methods are reactive, this session architects the next evolution: the Agentic BigQuery FinOps Framework, a platform designed to manage petabyte-scale costs. The key innovation is integrating Generative AI-powered agents, elevating the platform from a static reporting tool to an interactive, on-demand analytics service. sessions.t: Agentic Logistics: Powering GXO IQ with Google Cloud, Vertex AI, and Gemini
sessions.desc:
GXO, the world’s largest third-party, pure-play contract logistics provider, built GXO IQ to give customers enterprise‑grade logistics performance without enterprise‑level complexity. GXO IQ is an AI‑powered, agentic operating system that unifies billions of operational signals and decades of expertise into a single orchestration layer. Built on Google Cloud using GKE, Apigee, and Vertex AI with Gemini, it predicts demand, optimizes labor and pick paths, and triggers replenishment before orders drop. With an embedded conversational agent for real‑time insights, GXO IQ accelerates onboarding to weeks, reduces operational effort through automation, and delivers scalable end‑to‑end execution across the supply chain.

sessions.t: Agents in the CLI: Planning, delegation, and multi-agent execution
sessions.desc:
A lot of developers are trying agent-driven workflows in the terminal only to find some patterns don’t scale past small experiments. This talk examines which CLI-based agent patterns hold up in practice using GitHub Copilot CLI as a case study. We’ll look at how developers can plan work, delegate steps, and coordinate multi-step tasks while staying in control. We’ll also look at the tradeoffs that matters when deciding whether these workflows belong in your day-to-day development process (and when switching to your IDE may be more helpful).

sessions.t: AI Agents Across Clouds: Bridging Google Cloud AI and Third-Party Contact Centers for Healthcare Outreach
sessions.desc:
Effective healthcare outreach requires an AI agent that can navigate the reality of legacy telephony systems and multi-cloud environments. In this technical deep-dive, we showcase a production-ready architecture that bridges Google Cloud conversational AI with third-party contact center platforms to power secure, scalable, and HIPAA-compliant member outreach.
We will analyze the end-to-end architecture, highlighting how Google Cloud Cross-Cloud Interconnect enables secure connectivity while maintaining a strong security posture. You will learn how we engineered AI "playbooks" to manage complex conversational workflows, including seamless IVR-to-IVR communication and high-fidelity Answering Machine Detection (AMD). We will also demonstrate how the AI agent intelligently navigates external legacy IVR systems, automating workflows traditionally handled by human agents.

Attendees will leave with a reference architecture and practical design patterns for building secure, multi-cloud AI solutions for contact center automation and beyond.
sessions.q:
ai agents across clouds: bridging google cloud ai and third-party contact centers for healthcare outreach effective healthcare outreach requires an ai agent that can navigate the reality of legacy telephony systems and multi-cloud environments. in this technical deep-dive, we showcase a production-ready architecture that bridges google cloud conversational ai with third-party contact center platforms to power secure, scalable, and hipaa-compliant member outreach.
we will analyze the end-to-end architecture, highlighting how google cloud cross-cloud interconnect enables secure connectivity while maintaining a strong security posture. you will learn how we engineered ai "playbooks" to manage complex conversational workflows, including seamless ivr-to-ivr communication and high-fidelity answering machine detection (amd). we will also demonstrate how the ai agent intelligently navigates external legacy ivr systems, automating workflows traditionally handled by human agents.

sessions.t: AI at Snap's scale: Building a global compute fabric with GKE custom compute classes
sessions.desc: Hyperscale infrastructure hits a wall when integrating AI into global service meshes. To orchestrate obtainability-constrained accelerators, Snap and Google Cloud engineered a fundamental shift in Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) scaling. In this session, we’ll reveal the architecture of custom ComputeClasses, a feature born from Snap’s requirements that is now a standard part of GKE. Join us to learn to architect a mesh that mixes diverse AI compute resources to overcome supply constraints and scale workloads. And leave with the blueprint for optimizing performance and capacity at planetary scale on GKE.
sessions.q: ai at snap's scale: building a global compute fabric with gke custom compute classes hyperscale infrastructure hits a wall when integrating ai into global service meshes. to orchestrate obtainability-constrained accelerators, snap and google cloud engineered a fundamental shift in google kubernetes engine (gke) scaling. in this session, we’ll reveal the architecture of custom computeclasses, a feature born from snap’s requirements that is now a standard part of gke. join us to learn to architect a mesh that mixes diverse ai compute resources to overcome supply constraints and scale workloads. and leave with the blueprint for optimizing performance and capacity at planetary scale on gke. stas pereverziev snap shub shrivastava google cloud victor szalvay google cloud

sessions.t: AI at the edge: Transform operations with Google Distributed Cloud
sessions.desc: There are many advantages to AI at the edge: faster decision times, increased fault tolerance, reduced customer bandwidth requirements, lower total solution cost, and improved physical and data security. Come explore how organizations are transforming business operations at the edge with AI on Google Distributed Cloud (GDC). We’ll cover how GDC addresses the challenges associated with modernizing the edge, the power of Google Cloud regions – low latency, data privacy (regulatory, IP), retail locations, distribution centers, and manufacturing – and how to streamline your transition from cloud-based AI development to massive-scale edge deployment.
sessions.q: ai at the edge: transform operations with google distributed cloud there are many advantages to ai at the edge: faster decision times, increased fault tolerance, reduced customer bandwidth requirements, lower total solution cost, and improved physical and data security. come explore how organizations are transforming business operations at the edge with ai on google distributed cloud (gdc). we’ll cover how gdc addresses the challenges associated with modernizing the edge, the power of google cloud regions – low latency, data privacy (regulatory, ip), retail locations, distribution centers, and manufacturing – and how to streamline your transition from cloud-based ai development to massive-scale edge deployment. eric conn leverege sven gruenitz google cloud beatriz sebastian google cloud

sessions.t: AI Co-Pilot for Migrations: Deep Graph Assessment to Auto-Refactoring
sessions.desc: Manual assessment and refactoring consume massive engineering time and divert teams from strategic work during app and data migrations. This session unveils an end-to-end AI-assisted framework designed to slash this toil. First, see how MigSight uses static code analysis and a Graph RAG agent to build a deep knowledge graph of your code dependencies. Then, discover how MigConvert uses a human-in-the-loop approach, orchestrating deterministic tools and fine-tuned models to automate complex code conversion. Join us to learn how to seamlessly move from intelligent assessment to accurate code translation in your agent-driven modernization journey.
sessions.q: ai co-pilot for migrations: deep graph assessment to auto-refactoring manual assessment and refactoring consume massive engineering time and divert teams from strategic work during app and data migrations. this session unveils an end-to-end ai-assisted framework designed to slash this toil. first, see how migsight uses static code analysis and a graph rag agent to build a deep knowledge graph of your code dependencies. then, discover how migconvert uses a human-in-the-loop approach, orchestrating deterministic tools and fine-tuned models to automate complex code conversion. join us to learn how to seamlessly move from intelligent assessment to accurate code translation in your agent-driven modernization journey. vijay pandurengan google cloud

sessions.t: AI inference: Performance when you need it, economy when you don't
sessions.desc: As Generative AI moves from pilot to production, the 'one-size-fits-all' approach to serving is no longer viable. Platform teams face the dual challenge of meeting stringent SLOs for real-time applications while minimizing TCO for high-volume, cost-sensitive tasks. In this session, we unveil GKE Inference Gateway innovations designed to solve this equation. Explore how GKE delivers multi-accelerator flexibility and disaggregated serving for optimal price-performance. We’ll demonstrate how to fortify models using Model Armor, agent identity, and token-based quota protections. Finally, discover how to optimize inference with accelerators spread across multiple cloud regions

sessions.t: Always-on agentic life cycle: Connecting the development environment directly to production
sessions.desc: The traditional software development life cycle (SDLC) is linear, but the agentic SDLC is circular. We propose a harness-first approach that builds durable deep agents in production and connects them directly to Gemini CLI environments. This session demonstrates how a production agent running in a hardened harness with sub-agent swarms serves as a local collaborator. Using Model Context Protocol (MCP) and the Agent2Agent protocol, this approach brings live enterprise telemetry into your inner loop. Join this session to observe how we bootstrap a project from a production harness, use it to refactor code locally, and deploy changes that the agent then monitors in the background.
sessions.q: always-on agentic life cycle: connecting the development environment directly to production the traditional software development life cycle (sdlc) is linear, but the agentic sdlc is circular. we propose a harness-first approach that builds durable deep agents in production and connects them directly to gemini cli environments. this session demonstrates how a production agent running in a hardened harness with sub-agent swarms serves as a local collaborator. using model context protocol (mcp) and the agent2agent protocol, this approach brings live enterprise telemetry into your inner loop. join this session to observe how we bootstrap a project from a production harness, use it to refactor code locally, and deploy changes that the agent then monitors in the background. preston holmes google cloud todd segal google cloud andrey tapekha deutsche bank us tech center

sessions.t: Architecting Gemini Enterprise for regulated industries
sessions.desc: "Can we use GenAI without data leaving our perimeter?" For regulated industries, the answer must be a verifiable "Yes." This session whiteboards a Zero Trust GenAI Architecture using Gemini. We design the "Private RAG" pattern for confidential data, tackling three challenges: Perimeter, by configuring VPC-SC and PSC to block public internet traversal; Encryption, via CMEK and Data Residency for sovereignty; and Identity, by architecting ACL propagation to the Vector Database. Leave with a reference architecture that satisfies your CISO.

sessions.t: Beyond backup: What cyber recovery actually requires today
sessions.desc:
Legacy snapshots, custom scripts, and versioning weren't built for today's cyber threats. When attackers strike, can you identify the blast radius, verify your recovery point is clean, and restore operations with confidence? To actually recover your business, you need three critical capabilities: immutable copies that attackers can't delete, air-gapped storage they can't reach, and verified identities to access them. This session explores what true cyber recovery requires for organizations running critical Google workloads, such as Google Compute Engine, Google Cloud SQL, and Google Workspace. Leave with a framework to evaluate whether your organization is ready to recover from a cyber attack.

sessions.t: Build and optimize GKE workloads: A master class in node-level performance
sessions.desc: Maximizing performance requires deep node-level customization and precise benchmarking. In this discussion, we'll reveal configuration and tooling for optimizing stateless, stateful, and inference workloads on GKE. Discover new GKE APIs that generate pod manifests with industry-standard optimizations built-in. We'll dive deep into benchmarking and tuning Nginx, Redis, MySQL, and preview of GKE new Inference optimzation, while sharing a preview of packaged optimizations. You'll learn to leverage open-source frameworks to reproduce these gains for your unique workloads. Leave this discussion with a performance-tuning playbook and a reference architecture for high-scale GKE nodes.

sessions.t: Build beyond the CPU: Native AI and agentic autoscaling on GKE
sessions.desc: Standard autoscaling based on CPU and memory metrics often forces a choice: either over-provision resources or risk latency spikes during demand surges. Join this session for an introduction to intent-based autoscaling in Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE), a paradigm that adds automated rightsizing and custom workload metrics as native capabilities. Learn to architect native scaling based on application metrics such as queue depths, agent tool use, or latency histograms, eliminating the need for external adapters or sidecars. And discover how to move beyond generic resource limits to create a fluid infrastructure that reacts to real-time AI demand.
sessions.q: build beyond the cpu: native ai and agentic autoscaling on gke standard autoscaling based on cpu and memory metrics often forces a choice: either over-provision resources or risk latency spikes during demand surges. join this session for an introduction to intent-based autoscaling in google kubernetes engine (gke), a paradigm that adds automated rightsizing and custom workload metrics as native capabilities. learn to architect native scaling based on application metrics such as queue depths, agent tool use, or latency histograms, eliminating the need for external adapters or sidecars. and discover how to move beyond generic resource limits to create a fluid infrastructure that reacts to real-time ai demand. valentin hamburger google cloud adrian ludwin google cloud

sessions.t: Build multi-agent systems that actually work
sessions.desc:
Multi-agent architectures are powerful but over-applied. Join this session with Anthropic to learn when multiple agents genuinely outperform a single agent – and how to build them with Claude on Vertex AI. You’ll gain insight into the three scenarios where multi-agent consistently wins (context isolation, parallel execution, and specialization), the four architecture patterns with concrete trade-offs, and the context-centric decomposition strategy most teams get wrong. Plus, you’ll explore verification subagents and the pitfalls that undermine even well-designed systems.

sessions.t: Building multi-agent orchestration: Key challenges and solution approaches
sessions.desc: A single agent is useful, but coordinated teams are game-changers. Scaling multi-agent systems shifts the challenge from prompting to complex orchestration. This deep dive explores managing state, error handling, and task routing in production. We compare hierarchical, graph-based, and broadcast architectures, sharing battle-tested patterns for Google Cloud. Learn to implement robust recovery for chain failures, design efficient agent-to-agent communication, and secure tool-use via Vertex AI and Gemini for enterprise-scale deployment.

sessions.t: Building next-gen audio-first experiences: Lessons from PlayStation
sessions.desc: Speech is the next frontier of multimodal interaction, transforming how users engage with technology. Move beyond basic voice commands and discover what it takes to deploy low-latency, audio-first agents at global scale. In this session, we’ll break down how PlayStation partnered with Google Cloud to redefine their player experience, evolving from standard voice tech to an intelligent system that achieved a massive reduction in operational costs while significantly increasing caption accuracy. We’ll dive deep into the technical architecture behind these results, demonstrating how Gemini Audio on Vertex AI enables real-time multilingual speech-to-text (STT) support and hyper-natural text-to-speech (TTS) cues that improve accessibility for everyone. Whether you’re solving for immersive gaming or enterprise support, join us to get the production-ready blueprint for building voice interfaces that are faster, smarter, and more cost-effective.

sessions.desc: Go beyond the breakout demo and dive into the practical implementation of the new agentic developer lifecycle. Join this discussion to explore how to integrate the Data Engineering Agent and Airflow 3 into your daily engineering workflow. We will discuss best practices for "shifting left" with local testing in VS Code, setting up secure CI/CD pipelines, and optimizing DAG parsing performance. Bring your questions on using agents for root-cause analysis and share strategies for implementing deferrable operators to build cost-effective, self-healing pipelines at scale.

sessions.t: Building reliable agents
sessions.desc: The gap between a working demo and a production agent is architecture, not prompts. This talk examines the engineering discipline emerging around reliable agents: context engineering that treats memory as infrastructure, not afterthought; observability that traces multi-step reasoning chains; and constrained autonomy that favors narrow, specialized agents over ambitious generalists. Drawing from industry patterns and production failures, learn what it actually takes to ship agents that work at scale.
sessions.q: building reliable agents the gap between a working demo and a production agent is architecture, not prompts. this talk examines the engineering discipline emerging around reliable agents: context engineering that treats memory as infrastructure, not afterthought; observability that traces multi-step reasoning chains; and constrained autonomy that favors narrow, specialized agents over ambitious generalists. drawing from industry patterns and production failures, learn what it actually takes to ship agents that work at scale. muhammad farooq

sessions.t: Building the future of 3D worlds and interactive play with Gemini
sessions.desc:
As generative AI evolves from simple text outputs to complex, multi-modal environments, the boundaries of digital creativity are being rewritten. Join us for a high-energy lightning talk featuring two pioneers at the intersection of AI, gaming, and 3D animation: Latitude and Cartwheel.

In this session, you’ll discover how these innovators are leveraging the Gemini 3 family and Vertex AI to move from content generation to innovation:
Latitude: Learn how the creators of the world’s #1 AI Game Platform are building Voyage, their next-generation AI World Engine. Latitude will share how they use Gemini’s long-context and reasoning capabilities to orchestrate complex game logic, enabling "prompt-to-play" experiences where entire interactive worlds are built in real-time.

Cartwheel: See the future of motion with the team redefining 3D animation. Cartwheel will demonstrate their multi-modal pipeline that translates natural language to motion, video to motion, and applies complex 3D movements to characters instantly. Learn how Gemini powers their "Pose Mode" and 3D-native controls to provide professional-grade precision for animators and creators alike.

building the future of 3d worlds and interactive play with gemini as generative ai evolves from simple text outputs to complex, multi-modal environments, the boundaries of digital creativity are being rewritten. join us for a high-energy lightning talk featuring two pioneers at the intersection of ai, gaming, and 3d animation: latitude and cartwheel.

sessions.t: Building trust in AI: A discussion on agent security and governance
sessions.desc: What are your top security concerns when it comes to AI agents accessing enterprise data? What level of control and visibility do you need to feel comfortable? How do you balance the need for AI/agent democratization capabilities with the imperative for security and compliance? In this collaborative discussion, CISOs and IT executives will examine the multi-tiered security frameworks - integrating identity, network, and content layers - that are essential for securing agentic workflows. Through peer-to-peer dialogue, we will address top security risks and define the level of observability needed to maintain confidence in AI. Discussions will focus on practical methodologies for balancing rapid agent adoption with centralized governance, least-privilege access, and rigorous auditing. Join this group to share your perspective and contribute to the "enterprise-readiness checklist" for the future of AI-driven productivity.
sessions.q: building trust in ai: a discussion on agent security and governance what are your top security concerns when it comes to ai agents accessing enterprise data? what level of control and visibility do you need to feel comfortable? how do you balance the need for ai/agent democratization capabilities with the imperative for security and compliance? in this collaborative discussion, cisos and it executives will examine the multi-tiered security frameworks - integrating identity, network, and content layers - that are essential for securing agentic workflows. through peer-to-peer dialogue, we will address top security risks and define the level of observability needed to maintain confidence in ai. discussions will focus on practical methodologies for balancing rapid agent adoption with centralized governance, least-privilege access, and rigorous auditing. join this group to share your perspective and contribute to the "enterprise-readiness checklist" for the future of ai-driven productivity. dmitry pimenov google cloud

sessions.t: Building Trustable Modeling Agents Using a Neurosymbolic Approach
sessions.desc: Enterprise standards are too complex for traditional RAG. This session demonstrates a shift from simple documentation retrieval to Symbolic Anchoring. We’ll perform a technical deep-dive into a multi-agent orchestrated system designed for data modeling. We will explore advanced techniques—including mathematical label segregation and subgraph expansion—and show how a generative Actor-Critic loop provides the deterministic governance needed for enterprise trust. Discover how to enable the next generation of agents that reason, verify, and architect with scientific precision.
sessions.q: building trustable modeling agents using a neurosymbolic approach enterprise standards are too complex for traditional rag. this session demonstrates a shift from simple documentation retrieval to symbolic anchoring. we’ll perform a technical deep-dive into a multi-agent orchestrated system designed for data modeling. we will explore advanced techniques—including mathematical label segregation and subgraph expansion—and show how a generative actor-critic loop provides the deterministic governance needed for enterprise trust. discover how to enable the next generation of agents that reason, verify, and architect with scientific precision. jerome rajan google

sessions.t: Choice and control: Unified access and routing with Vertex AI
sessions.desc: Production AI requires orchestrating diverse models while managing sprawl and cost. Join this session to explore a unified architecture for scaling your AI. We’ll start by diving into Model Garden on Vertex AI to deploy Gemini, Claude, and OSS models. Then, we’ll operationalize them using model routing with API Gateway. And we’ll demonstrate how to create a single endpoint that lets developers explicitly switch between models via configuration, establishing the foundation for future intelligent optimization and cost control.
sessions.q: choice and control: unified access and routing with vertex ai production ai requires orchestrating diverse models while managing sprawl and cost. join this session to explore a unified architecture for scaling your ai. we’ll start by diving into model garden on vertex ai to deploy gemini, claude, and oss models. then, we’ll operationalize them using model routing with api gateway. and we’ll demonstrate how to create a single endpoint that lets developers explicitly switch between models via configuration, establishing the foundation for future intelligent optimization and cost control. mak ahmad google cloud matt chesler two sigma chase lyall google cloud

sessions.t: Choose the right stack for Go agents
sessions.desc: Choosing the right abstraction layer for an AI agent is critical. We’ll compare code complexity and maintainability to help you understand the trade-offs of each method and decide when to use each one.
sessions.q: choose the right stack for go agents choosing the right abstraction layer for an ai agent is critical. join this session to code the same agent in go using three different approaches: from scratch, with genkit, and with google’s agent development kit (adk). we’ll compare code complexity and maintainability to help you understand the trade-offs of each method and decide when to use each one. marc dougherty google cloud

sessions.desc: We’ve all heard the story that the browser is the new operating system, right? Laptops provided.

sessions.t: CISO Connect: Security in the Next Era
sessions.desc:
CISO Connect is an exclusive gathering of your peers for a day of high-level strategy and candid conversation. This isn’t just another track; it’s a deep dive into the initiatives shaping our industry.

Hear directly from Google’s security leadership—including Francis deSouza, Sandra Joyce, Royal Hansen and Heather Adkins —alongside fellow CISOs navigating the same front lines as you.

What we’re tackling:


Intelligence-Led Defense: Gain fresh visibility into the modern threat landscape and learn how to translate front-line intelligence into executive action.
The Evolution of the SOC: Exploring the shift toward "Agentic" security and how autonomous capabilities are redefining incident response and operational scale.
Architecting for the Future: A strategic look at building resilient systems that can withstand the convergence of AI, quantum computing, and a hyper-connected global infrastructure.
Peer Leadership & Resilience: Candid discussions with top CISOs on driving a security culture that enables—rather than hinders—rapid business growth.
sessions.q:
ciso connect: security in the next era ciso connect is an exclusive gathering of your peers for a day of high-level strategy and candid conversation. this isn’t just another track; it’s a deep dive into the initiatives shaping our industry.

hear directly from google’s security leadership—including francis desouza, sandra joyce, royal hansen and heather adkins —alongside fellow cisos navigating the same front lines as you.

what we’re tackling:


intelligence-led defense: gain fresh visibility into the modern threat landscape and learn how to translate front-line intelligence into executive action.
the evolution of the soc: exploring the shift toward "agentic" security and how autonomous capabilities are redefining incident response and operational scale.
architecting for the future: a strategic look at building resilient systems that can withstand the convergence of ai, quantum computing, and a hyper-connected global infrastructure.
peer leadership & resilience: candid discussions with top cisos on driving a security culture that enables—rather than hinders—rapid business growth.

sessions.t: Cloud Native SASE with Fortinet and Google Unified Security
sessions.desc:
Ensuring your hybrid workforce stays secure shouldn't mean sacrificing performance or visibility. By combining Fortinet FortiSASE with Google Security Operations, your team gains a unified networking and security hub that scales globally.

Rather than juggling disjointed tools, you get low-latency connectivity via Google’s private backbone and a single pane of glass for continuous threat detection. This integration lets you correlate network events with security incidents in real-time, speeding up remediation and protecting your hybrid estate.



By attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow up for this event only.

sessions.t: Cloud Storage Rapid: Turbocharged object storage for AI and analytics
sessions.desc: Traditional object storage is built for scale, not speed. Why not have both? Cloud Storage Rapid is a high-performance tier of object storage built for intensive workloads like data preparation, training, and inference. Used by some of the world’s largest AI and ML customers, Cloud Storage Rapid (including Anywhere Cache and Rapid Bucket) provides terabyte-scale throughput and submillisecond latency while maintaining simplicity, scale, and cost-effectiveness. Join us to dissect real-world architectures where feeding compute clusters directly from Google Cloud Storage maximizes graphics processing unit, tensor processing unit, and data management utilization while reducing overall complexity.
sessions.q: cloud storage rapid: turbocharged object storage for ai and analytics traditional object storage is built for scale, not speed. why not have both? cloud storage rapid is a high-performance tier of object storage built for intensive workloads like data preparation, training, and inference. used by some of the world’s largest ai and ml customers, cloud storage rapid (including anywhere cache and rapid bucket) provides terabyte-scale throughput and submillisecond latency while maintaining simplicity, scale, and cost-effectiveness. join us to dissect real-world architectures where feeding compute clusters directly from google cloud storage maximizes graphics processing unit, tensor processing unit, and data management utilization while reducing overall complexity. joachim hammer google cloud vivek saraswat google cloud james sun thinking machines lab

sessions.t: Code-to-Cloud visibility: The new foundation for securing the AI-DLC
sessions.desc: Code-to-cloud visibility isn’t a feature; it’s the foundation for secure development in the AI era. As the velocity of engineering teams increases with the AI-native development lifecycle, the disconnect between where risks are introduced (code) and where they manifest (cloud) leads to missed ownership and slow remediation. This session explores how development and AppSec teams can prioritize based on whether a code flaw is actually reachable or exploitable in production. See a live demo of how code-to-cloud context can be embedded directly into the AI-DLC to start secure by default.
sessions.q: code-to-cloud visibility: the new foundation for securing the ai-dlc code-to-cloud visibility isn’t a feature; it’s the foundation for secure development in the ai era. as the velocity of engineering teams increases with the ai-native development lifecycle, the disconnect between where risks are introduced (code) and where they manifest (cloud) leads to missed ownership and slow remediation. this session explores how development and appsec teams can prioritize based on whether a code flaw is actually reachable or exploitable in production. see a live demo of how code-to-cloud context can be embedded directly into the ai-dlc to start secure by default. lucas gomes wiz salman ladha wiz

sessions.t: Complex workflows made easy with Gemini Enterprise agents
sessions.desc: Step into the future of enterprise productivity with an in depth exploration of how Gemini Enterprise transforms complex industry workflows into simple and easy to manage agents. In this session, we'll show you how to register and use agents built with the modern agent stack – including Agent Development Kit (ADK), Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocols, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and more – on Gemini Enterprise. sessions.t: Configure Vector Search in AlloyDB
sessions.desc: Unlock semantic search with AlloyDB and Vertex AI. AlloyDB is a fully managed, PostgreSQL-compatible database service designed for your most demanding workloads. In this introductory-level workshop, you will implement vector search to find data based on meaning rather than exact keywords—ideal for complex inputs. You’ll learn to configure an AlloyDB database, load data, and generate text embeddings. Finally, you will execute fast similarity searches to identify related content, empowering your applications with AI-driven retrieval capabilities.

sessions.t: Connect to Remote Agents with ADK and the Agent2Agent (A2A) SDK
sessions.desc: Bridge the gap between diverse AI frameworks with the Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol. A2A creates a common language for agents to collaborate across servers. In this advanced workshop, you will deploy an Agent Development Kit (ADK) agent as an A2A Server and define its capabilities using a JSON Agent Card. Then, you will configure a second agent to discover and control the first as a sub-agent. Master the standards for agent discovery, rich data exchange, and seamless interoperability in this hands-on session.

sessions.t: Context engineering for agents
sessions.desc:
Context engineering is the iterative process of curating exactly the information an agent needs —- no more, no less -- inside its context window to guide what it does next. A well-engineered context window is often what enables agents to succeed in the wild. In this talk, we’ll cover common techniques and best practices for building effective contexts, and show how MongoDB provides all the components needed to deliver precisely the right information to your agents.







By attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow up for this event only.

sessions.t: Context matters: Sensitive Data Protection and data security
sessions.desc: When it comes to protecting data, especially in critical AI workloads, context is key. Understanding context helps you figure out when you’re dealing with sensitive or unwanted data that needs protection. In this session, you’ll learn how Sensitive Data Protection looks beyond simple keyword matches and uses advanced AI and ML, bringing in new context classifications (such as medical and finance) and image object and theme detection (such as person or face, passports, or photo ID cards) to help better identify and classify data.
sessions.q: context matters: sensitive data protection and data security when it comes to protecting data, especially in critical ai workloads, context is key. understanding context helps you figure out when you’re dealing with sensitive or unwanted data that needs protection. in this session, you’ll learn how sensitive data protection looks beyond simple keyword matches and uses advanced ai and ml, bringing in new context classifications (such as medical and finance) and image object and theme detection (such as person or face, passports, or photo id cards) to help better identify and classify data. scott ellis google cloud ivan medvedev google cloud

sessions.t: Control the swarm: how to simulate your operations by leveraging digital twins
sessions.desc: The modern warehouse is no longer a solo act—it’s a chaotic mix of humans, AI agents, and heterogeneous robot fleets from different vendors. The challenge isn't just automation; it's multi-agent orchestration. In this lightning talk, discover how industry leaders leverage Google Cloud to power digital twins that simulate their entire operations. We’ll explore how this agentic architecture processes millions of operations, assigning the right task to the right agent—whether it’s a human picker, a third-party AMR, or a conveyor system—in real-time. Hear about the blueprint for building a digital nervous system that adapts instantly to traffic, demand spikes, and resource availability.

sessions.t: CPU infrastructure for the age of inference: Lessons from industry leaders
sessions.desc: General-purpose compute is emerging as the critical engine for the age of inference. Join this session to learn firsthand from industry leaders who have successfully implemented a workload-optimized approach to power their AI stacks. We’ll cover topics ranging from high-efficiency CPU-based inference to the massive data preprocessing required for modern generative workloads. Learn how to build an AI-ready infrastructure that eliminates bottlenecks and minimizes latency across your entire pipeline. We’ll break down the emerging architectural patterns that provide the flexibility to innovate at scale, so your infrastructure can scale seamlessly without being locked into a single path.

sessions.t: Cryptographic proof: Ensuring confidentiality, sovereignty, and control
sessions.desc: In the era of AI, security must move beyond simple policy to verifiable cryptographic proof. Join us to explore the next generation of trusted infrastructure designed to protect your most sensitive assets without inhibiting innovation. We will unveil Confidential External Key Manager, enabling you to maintain possession and control of your keys in a highly performant, verifiable, confidential environment. We’ll also demonstrate single-tenant Cloud HSM, a new standard for cryptographic isolation that grants you absolute control over dedicated HSM clusters without operational toil. Finally, you’ll discover how we’re simplifying compliance for regulated enterprises with Cloud HSM for Google Workspace and delivering new resiliency capabilities for Secret Manager.

sessions.t: Demystifying evals at the frontier of agentic development
sessions.desc:
Teams without evals get stuck in reactive loops—catching issues in production, unable to distinguish regressions from noise. Teams that invest early find development accelerates as failures become test cases and metrics replace guesswork. This talk shares what Anthropic has learned building evals for Claude Code and deploying them with customers across coding, conversational, and research agents. You'll leave with a roadmap: structuring agent evals, choosing the right graders, balancing capability vs. regression testing, and building a suite you trust.

sessions.t: Director's Cut: How Google GenAI is Scripting the Future of Film
sessions.desc:
This session explores the rise of GenMedia as a core pillar of modern film/movie/media production, offering a deep dive into how Google’s Generative AI models are being deployed at scale to help creators realize previously impossible cinematic visions.

Our analysis focuses on the engineering of the frame, specifically detailing the specialized generative super-resolution and outpainting techniques used to intelligently widen aspect ratios and upscale source material. By synthesizing contextually accurate visual data, these tools allow filmmakers to expand their original narrative canvas into larger and wider canvases without compromising the original cinematography or narrative integrity. Attendees will gain insight into how Google’s unique AI infrastructure enables a truly next-level viewing experience, scaling pixels and perspectives to meet the rigorous demands of the world's most advanced digital theaters and premium large-format screens.
sessions.q:

sessions.t: FinOps after the honeymoon: Managing cloud costs at real scale
sessions.desc:
FinOps success often starts with dramatic early wins, but the hardest work begins once the low-hanging fruit is gone. In this session, Amit Kinha draws from real-world FinOps leadership to explore what breaks down after the honeymoon period: delayed cost data, fear of automation, reactive governance, and the “cost police” trap. Attendees will learn how mature teams shift FinOps upstream, align cost with product decisions, and move from firefighting to proactive, outcome-driven cloud financial management.







By attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow up for this event only.

A mid-size enterprise security team receives 10,000 alerts a day, but has capacity to investigate maybe 50. Which ones do they choose?

Come learn about a different approach to security operations that isn’t adding more analysts, but rather rethinking your strategy from the ground up.

TENEX is AI-native MDR that offers AI agents that handle 95% of alert volume autonomously, while human analysts focus on the 5% that requires judgment and customer context. This is a human-led operation where your existing team becomes dramatically more effective.

sessions.t: Generative UI for any agent, anywhere: A2UI, AG-UI, MCP Apps, and more
sessions.desc: The future of the web isn’t just interactive – it’s adaptive. Break the “chat wall” with agents that customize UIs for any context. Join the core teams behind A2UI, a native-first standard that treats UI as declarative data for ultimate security and performance, establishing your design system as the “trust catalog.” This session also features CopilotKit’s CEO discussing AG-UI, which connects all frontends and agent backends, and one of MCP Apps’ co-creators promoting agent UI standardization everywhere. sessions.t: Give your team an agent that works 24/7 and actually fixes things
sessions.desc:
Most cloud tools are good at reporting issues like vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and drift. There’s a dashboard for each. But dashboards don’t close tickets. Teams still spend nights fixing issues instead of building what matters.
This session shows how Cast AI’s agentic workflows turn findings into fixes. The system identifies issues across infrastructure, applications, and databases, validates changes in context, and proposes fixes as pull requests, with engineers stepping in at completion, not every step, reducing operational toil and freeing teams to focus on higher-impact work.

sessions.t: GKE efficiency master class: Architecting for optimal price-performance
sessions.desc: This technical roundtable moves beyond basic right-sizing to deconstruct deep-stack GKE optimization. We lead a focused discussion on architecting high-density clusters using Secondary Boot Disks, custom machine types, and Advanced Bin-Packing to maximize hardware saturation. Bring your specific scale challenges as we debate the trade-offs of spot-preemption strategies for AI workloads and the impact of multidimensional pod autoscaling on your bottom line. Walk away with a peer-vetted optimization blueprint and a reference architecture for maintaining extreme efficiency at planetary scale.
sessions.q: gke efficiency master class: architecting for optimal price-performance this technical roundtable moves beyond basic right-sizing to deconstruct deep-stack gke optimization. we lead a focused discussion on architecting high-density clusters using secondary boot disks, custom machine types, and advanced bin-packing to maximize hardware saturation. bring your specific scale challenges as we debate the trade-offs of spot-preemption strategies for ai workloads and the impact of multidimensional pod autoscaling on your bottom line. walk away with a peer-vetted optimization blueprint and a reference architecture for maintaining extreme efficiency at planetary scale. emeka nwafor google cloud

sessions.t: GKE supercluster: Powering secure, planetary scale for AI workloads
sessions.desc: Discover the future of secure AI, co-designed by Google and Anthropic. This session unveils GKE supercluster, a new Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) architecture that decouples the control plane from the data plane to shatter scaling limits, managing over one million accelerator chips as a single, unified pool. The Titanium Intelligence Enclave delivers hardware-attested, "sealed vault" security, ensuring proprietary AI model weights remain cryptographically invisible to cloud providers and platform admins. You'll learn how to secure and train the world's most demanding AI models at scale.
sessions.q: gke supercluster: powering secure, planetary scale for ai workloads discover the future of secure ai, co-designed by google and anthropic. this session unveils gke supercluster, a new google kubernetes engine (gke) architecture that decouples the control plane from the data plane to shatter scaling limits, managing over one million accelerator chips as a single, unified pool. the titanium intelligence enclave delivers hardware-attested, "sealed vault" security, ensuring proprietary ai model weights remain cryptographically invisible to cloud providers and platform admins. you'll learn how to secure and train the world's most demanding ai models at scale. greg castle google cloud glen messenger google cloud

sessions.t: Govern your agents: Architecting a secure agentic ecosystem with Vertex AI
sessions.desc: As AI agents gain more autonomy, the stakes for security and compliance are higher than ever. This session is essential for business leaders or IT decision-makers who need to balance rapid innovation with uncompromising security. Explore how to apply core principles of identity, authentication, and authorization to the new world of agents and tools, and how to adopt more advanced agent management capabilities as you scale. Learn how to use centralized control planes, granular access control lists (ACLs), and observability dashboards to maintain a ubiquitous view of your AI ecosystem. And discover how Vertex AI and Google Cloud’s integrated security tools allow you to stay in control and confident, proving that robust governance isn’t a bottleneck – it’s the engine that allows your organization to move faster safely.

sessions.t: Governing agentic state: Architect auditable loans with HITL on Vertex AI
sessions.desc: High-stakes financial workflows (e.g., loan underwriting) require a robust Pause/Resume Pattern for Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) to support Human-In-The-Loop (HITL) intervention. This session details an architecture using Vertex AI Agent Engine as the orchestrator and Cloud Run for execution. We cover how to manage state with Cloud Firestore, decouple governance via Eventarc to prevent timeouts, and use OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions for auditing agent rationale and human decisions for Responsible AI compliance.
sessions.q: governing agentic state: architect auditable loans with hitl on vertex ai high-stakes financial workflows (e.g., loan underwriting) require a robust pause/resume pattern for multi-agent systems (mas) to support human-in-the-loop (hitl) intervention. this session details an architecture using vertex ai agent engine as the orchestrator and cloud run for execution. we cover how to manage state with cloud firestore, decouple governance via eventarc to prevent timeouts, and use opentelemetry semantic conventions for auditing agent rationale and human decisions for responsible ai compliance. parag doshi google

sessions.t: Long-Running Agents: The AI that never sleeps
sessions.desc:
Today's AI agents are impressive but often don't run for long - they forget context mid-task, hallucinate when sessions get long, and declare victory before the job is done. What if an agent could work autonomously for hours or even days, maintaining state, recovering from failures, and actually shipping results?

In this session, we'll explore the architecture and patterns behind long-running agents — persistent AI workers that go far beyond single-prompt interactions. Through two live demos built on Google ADK and Cloud Run, you'll see a 24/7 competitive intelligence agent that monitors competitors across Reddit, YouTube, Hacker News, and the web (with historical trend detection powered by Firestore), and a creative coding agent that transformed a large Blender scene into a browser-explorable 3D environment in a single continuous session.

We'll cover the key engineering breakthroughs that make this possible — agent harnesses, persistent memory patterns, self-verification loops, and how Agent Engine's Sessions and Memory Bank provide managed infrastructure for agent persistence at scale. You'll leave with practical patterns for building agents that don't just answer questions, but plan, execute, debug, iterate, and ship.
sessions.q:
long-running agents: the ai that never sleeps today's ai agents are impressive but often don't run for long - they forget context mid-task, hallucinate when sessions get long, and declare victory before the job is done. what if an agent could work autonomously for hours or even days, maintaining state, recovering from failures, and actually shipping results?

we'll cover the key engineering breakthroughs that make this possible — agent harnesses, persistent memory patterns, self-verification loops, and how agent engine's sessions and memory bank provide managed infrastructure for agent persistence at scale. you'll leave with practical patterns for building agents that don't just answer questions, but plan, execute, debug, iterate, and ship. addy osmani google cloud

sessions.t: Modern observability for Cloud Run with Datadog
sessions.desc:
Serverless architectures on Google Cloud are easy to deploy and notoriously hard to debug. Because Cloud Run services typically operate within distributed systems, pinpointing regressions requires understanding how requests traverse multiple components. The evidence needed for end-to-end troubleshooting exists within a rich ecosystem of logs, metrics, and traces; the key challenge lies in correlating this telemetry across services.
In this session we’ll showcase Datadog’s unified observability for Google Cloud Run in action. See deployment markers layered directly onto performance data, monitor related Cloud Run Jobs, and follow distributed traces through Pub/Sub and beyond—so you can pinpoint impact, isolate root cause, and resolve production issues faster.

sessions.t: Patterns of successful AI agents
sessions.desc: Most agents demo well and fail in production. After seeing hundreds of agents up close and studying what actually ships, clear patterns emerge that separate agents that reliably get the job done from those that don't. This talk covers design patterns and techniques for production agents: everything from memory architectures that maintain coherence, tool design that outweighs model selection, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, verification loops that catch errors long before users do, graceful failure recovery, and evaluation strategies when ground truth doesn't exist. Practical, implementation-ready, no hype
sessions.q: patterns of successful ai agents most agents demo well and fail in production. after seeing hundreds of agents up close and studying what actually ships, clear patterns emerge that separate agents that reliably get the job done from those that don't. this talk covers design patterns and techniques for production agents: everything from memory architectures that maintain coherence, tool design that outweighs model selection, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, verification loops that catch errors long before users do, graceful failure recovery, and evaluation strategies when ground truth doesn't exist. practical, implementation-ready, no hype sam witteveen red dragon ai

sessions.t: Resilience everywhere across Google Cloud, On-Prem, and Edge
sessions.desc:
Organizations using Google Cloud have data that spans cloud,on-prem, and edge environments. However, resilience plans often stop at the cloud boundary, increasing the threat posed from a destructive cyberattack. This lightning talk examines common failure modes like ransomware, misconfiguration,
and accidental loss, and discusses architectural approaches to backup, immutability, incident response and recovery across complex enterprise IT environments. Attendees will leave with a practical resilience model that delivers guidance for risk reduction pre-attack, and best practices for clean, fast recovery post-attack.

sessions.t: Scaling inference for reasoning models & agents with NVIDIA on GKE
sessions.desc:
As AI models scale beyond single-GPU capacity, the gap between peak hardware performance and real-world serving widens. NVIDIA Dynamo is an open-source inference framework for large-scale serving, purpose-built to close that gap. By combining inference optimizations - like disaggregated inference and agentic-routing - with production-grade methods, Dynamo maximizes GPU utilization on Google Cloud’s A4X Max, powered by NVIDIA GB300 NVL72.

In this session, we'll discuss deploying Dynamo on Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) and share real-world benchmarks.

in this session, we'll discuss deploying dynamo on google kubernetes engine (gke) and share real-world benchmarks.

sessions.t: Secure and fast Agentic AI development with Gemini and GitLab
sessions.desc:
What happens when you let Gemini Pro and GitLab drive your entire development workflow? This live demo begins with a single prompt that generates a UI design, transforms it into working code inside Google Antigravity, then triggers a GitLab issue via MCP server integration. From there, a Gemini-powered external AI agent in GitLab takes over, reviewing the code and opening a merge request autonomously. Minutes later, a production-ready Python app lands. No handoffs. No bottlenecks. Just secure AI-orchestrated development from idea to deployment.

sessions.t: Securely connect your global enterprise with Cloud WAN
sessions.desc: Managing disparate wide area network (WAN) infrastructure across global sites is costly and complex, often resulting in poor performance, a high total cost of ownership, and gaps in security, not to mention it hampers business agility. Enterprises need a unified, high-speed, globally available WAN solution with integrated best-in-class security. Explore the latest innovations with Cloud WAN, part of Google Cloud’s Cross-Cloud Network. Cloud WAN is designed to simplify enterprise connectivity using Google’s global network. We’ll discuss new capabilities, ecosystem partnerships, and coverage expansions that allow you to leverage Google’s planet-scale network to connect your global enterprise with reduced latency, lower total cost of ownership, and higher security.
