sessions.t: Securing agent traffic at scale: Network enforcement for the Agentic Enterprise
sessions.desc:
As enterprises deploy autonomous AI agents into production, security must extend beyond identity and runtime controls to govern how agents communicate across cloud environments. In the Agentic Era, the network becomes the enforcement fabric that translates AI intent into safe execution—controlling traffic, inspecting behavior, and containing blast radius at machine speed. This session introduces a Network Security Control Plane for agentic architectures on Google Cloud. Attendees will learn how cloud-native network security enables real-time inspection of agent-to-service and east-west traffic, delivers Layer 7 visibility for AI workloads, and enforces consistent policy across environments. We will explore architectural patterns using Cloud NGFW, Google Cloud Network Security Integration, and managed, consumption-based firewall services to secure agentic workflows at scale without increasing operational complexity. Attendees will leave with a practical framework for integrating network security into the AI lifecycle alongside identity, runtime protection, and observability—turning network security into an execution guardrail for autonomous systems.



By attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow up for this event only.

securing agent traffic at scale: network enforcement for the agentic enterprise as enterprises deploy autonomous ai agents into production, security must extend beyond identity and runtime controls to govern how agents communicate across cloud environments. in the agentic era, the network becomes the enforcement fabric that translates ai intent into safe execution—controlling traffic, inspecting behavior, and containing blast radius at machine speed. this session introduces a network security control plane for agentic architectures on google cloud. attendees will learn how cloud-native network security enables real-time inspection of agent-to-service and east-west traffic, delivers layer 7 visibility for ai workloads, and enforces consistent policy across environments. we will explore architectural patterns using cloud ngfw, google cloud network security integration, and managed, consumption-based firewall services to secure agentic workflows at scale without increasing operational complexity. attendees will leave with a practical framework for integrating network security into the ai lifecycle alongside identity, runtime protection, and observability—turning network security into an execution guardrail for autonomous systems.

sessions.t: Securing AI-Generated Code: What's Actually Working?
sessions.desc: AI coding tools have fundamentally changed how software gets built. Developers are prompting instead of coding. Agents are committing autonomously. Code volume is accelerating faster than any security team can manually review. But most software security programs were designed for a world where humans wrote every line. In this interactive discussion, we'll have an honest conversation about how to maintain visibility, prioritize what actually matters, and what a modern AppSec program should look like. Come ready to share what's working, what isn't, and what you're still figuring out.
sessions.q: securing ai-generated code: what's actually working? ai coding tools have fundamentally changed how software gets built. developers are prompting instead of coding. agents are committing autonomously. code volume is accelerating faster than any security team can manually review. but most software security programs were designed for a world where humans wrote every line. in this interactive discussion, we'll have an honest conversation about how to maintain visibility, prioritize what actually matters, and what a modern appsec program should look like. come ready to share what's working, what isn't, and what you're still figuring out. salman ladha wiz

sessions.t: Securing AI: Real-world lessons and a roadmap for proactive defense
sessions.desc: Deploying large language models and AI agents introduces new security risks that require a modern approach to governance. In this session, we’ll share practical insights from the last year of securing production AI systems. You’ll learn how to identify common vulnerabilities and implement proactive measures like AI-specific threat modeling and red teaming. We’ll cover how to build a resilient response framework that protects your organization while accelerating AI adoption. Join us to get a clear roadmap for secure AI innovation.

sessions.t: Security for non-deterministic AI
sessions.desc: WAFs and DLP use deterministic matching, but LLMs are probabilistic. Join this technical roundtable to debate engineering safety when prompts yield varying outputs. We'll discuss stochastic detection trade-offs, Model Armor latency, and fail-safe logic in agentic loops. Swap strategies on shifting from binary blocking to probabilistic risk management in production.
sessions.q: security for non-deterministic ai wafs and dlp use deterministic matching, but llms are probabilistic. join this technical roundtable to debate engineering safety when prompts yield varying outputs. we'll discuss stochastic detection trade-offs, model armor latency, and fail-safe logic in agentic loops. swap strategies on shifting from binary blocking to probabilistic risk management in production.

sessions.t: Seismic shifts & bottom lines: Managing global fracture & AI-driven cyber risk
sessions.desc:
No longer an episodic concern, geopolitical instability and AI-driven cyber threats create persistent challenges that demand urgent C-suite attention. In this session, PwC’s Morgan Adamski and Google’s Sandra Joyce analyze how geopolitical fracturing will impact threat trends, how adversaries weaponize AI to scale attacks, and how companies can counter with the same technologies for their defense. Bridging intelligence and frontline response, they’ll share strategies for executives on assessing risk and building organization-wide resilience in this new world.

sessions.t: Takeaways from major open source library attacks
sessions.desc:
Open source library breaches have made one thing clear: malicious actors are weaponizing trust. Recent malware attacks bypassed detection tools by exploiting the gap between verifiable source code and the binaries pulled from public registries. This session breaks down what happened and how teams can move forward safely. We’ll explore Chainguard Libraries, which delivers rebuilt libraries from verifiable source with strong provenance, SBOMs, and security backports—closing with a demo and live Q&A.

sessions.t: Teach agents in the shadows: Incremental AI adoption with OpenTelemetry
sessions.desc: The strangler fig pattern enabled monolith-to-microservices migration. The cognitive strangler applies the same incremental approach to agent adoption. In this session, we’ll demonstrate two techniques: First, shadow agent deployment, where an agent runs in parallel with production calls through OpenTelemetry context propagation, generating responses without affecting the critical path. And second, the trace-to-training pipeline that exports span data as JSON for fine-tuning in Vertex AI.

sessions.t: The 2026 AI threat landscape
sessions.desc: In the past few months, LLMs have made a significant leap in offensive cybersecurity capability. As AI adoption accelerates across cloud environments, new threat patterns are emerging - often layered on top of the same familiar security failures attackers have exploited for years. This session examines how AI is reshaping attacker behavior, and where risk is concentrating in the cloud as the AI-driven attack surface expands, especially through vibe coding and agent-based workflows. Using real-world attacks and recently exploited vulnerabilities, we’ll highlight what’s changing, what isn’t, and what security teams should prioritize next.

sessions.t: The agents among us
sessions.desc:
As AI agents grow more autonomous, we need to know if we can trust them. Agents can be unpredictable, which raises the stakes on every decision they make. This session will explore how to build that trust through the monitoring and iteration of various LLMs – namely Google Gemini models – available via Vertex AI. We’ll examine how observability reveals agent reasoning, while controlled experiments and LLM-as-a-judge frameworks enable scalable evaluation. By implementing continuous evaluation, developers can create a safety net for change – transforming opacity into confidence using Datadog’s observability ecosystem.

sessions.t: The priority paradox: Navigating multi-tenant AI on GKE
sessions.desc: What happens when your high-priority RL training job and your mission-critical LLM inference spike hit the same cluster at the same time? Building a multi-tenant AI platform is a game of constant compromise. In this session, we’ll go beyond the "perfect" reference architecture to discuss the messy reality of multi-cluster orchestration. We'll share peer-to-peer insights on setting preemption policies that don’t alienate researchers, managing "goodput" during traffic surges, and the architectural shifts required to turn a collection of Kubernetes clusters into a unified, efficient AI factory.

sessions.t: The self-curation engine: Real-time schema mapping with Gemini and Cloud Run
sessions.desc: Retail data arrives from disparate sources with inconsistent schemas. To solve this, we present a serverless Cloud Run agent that uses the Gemini API to automate schema mapping. Triggered by new data, the agent compares raw schemas to a BigQuery target and dynamically generates the required SQL or Dataform transformations. This talk highlights how AI automates the most manual parts of data engineering: schema maintenance and logic generation.

sessions.t: Top 10 things we don't want you to know about Nano Banana
sessions.desc: Join us at Google Cloud Next as we reveal the "Top 10 things we don't want you to know about Nano Banana"—Google’s multimodal image generation and editing model. In this session, we’ll highlight Nano Banana's lesser-known advanced capabilities. sessions.t: TPU inference: Portability, cost, and scaling for state of the art
sessions.desc: Demystifying TPU inference doesn't have to require writing custom compilers. Join this collaborative discussion to exchange strategies for deploying vLLM and other frameworks across Google Cloud hardware. We will dissect real-world implementations, debating the business tradeoffs between time-to-first-token latency and raw throughput, the reality of switching workloads between GPUs and TPUs, and overcoming the production friction of cold-starts and OOMs. Bring your deployment headaches and swap real-world battle stories with fellow ML engineers and platform architects.

sessions.t: Trading in the cloud: How capital markets future-proof their infrastructure
sessions.desc: FSI and capital markets face a unique challenge, where trading infrastructure capacity is heavily dependent on economic factors. Financial exchanges must offer open and fair access to markets while providing determinism and regulatory compliance. Join this session and discuss with executives from CME Group and Eurex/DBG how Google Cloud supports their financial markets by providing exchanges and trading firms with highly deterministic compute, AI infrastructure, precision time, cloud-based peering, market data feeds, and observability.

sessions.t: Turn developer feedback into content recommendations with Gemini
sessions.desc: Go behind the scenes of a Google Cloud case study using Gemini to decode developer feedback from bugs, support tickets, and social channels. sessions.t: Unified Iceberg: Open standards, native performance
sessions.desc: Managing Apache Iceberg manifests, optimizing file sizes, and handling compaction all drain engineering resources. We’re solving this friction with a whole new set of features in BigLake designed to help you keep the open source compatibility you need while offloading the hard work to Google’s infrastructure. Join this session for a deep dive into the new, unified BigLake tables that help you build and optimize an open, enterprise-ready lakehouse without compromises and provide a seamless read/write interoperability between BigQuery and OSS engines.

sessions.t: Unified management plane for OTel: Server-side data control and processing
sessions.desc: OpenTelemetry (OTel) is the standard for observability, but enterprises often feel forced to choose between the reliability and scale of hyperscalers and the true open data models OpenTelemetry promises. In this session, we’ll introduce Telemetry Router, a managed solution that acts as the missing management plane for enterprise observability. Learn how to move beyond complex client-side management to achieve centralized data control, server-side transformation, and true data ownership. And take away a blueprint for scaling OTel without vendor lock-in.

sessions.t: Unlock capital with AI: Cardinal Health's high-stakes success
sessions.desc: AI offers a powerful new way to transform finance operations and unlock value. See how KPMG LLP and Cardinal Health are applying it to pricing disputes with a game-changing agent on Google Cloud. It helps transform hours of manual discovery into an overnight “dispute dossier,” empowering analysts with instant insights. In this session, learn how to slash resolution times, boost team capacity, and unlock working capital – all while keeping humans in control of the final decisions. This is high-stakes AI in action. sessions.t: Unlock enterprise data and drive AI adoption with Sensitive Data Protection
sessions.desc: Without the right safeguards and controls, AI agents and applications can put your most important data at risk. Join this technical session to demystify our latest data security solutions. You’ll discover how to automatically identify and redact sensitive information in AI training data, secure datasets with customer-managed encryption and security perimeters, and filter traffic in Gemini Enterprise and Vertex AI to prevent high-value data leakage. You’ll also learn how to use data security posture management in Security Command Center to govern the storage, movement, and processing of data across your entire AI lifecycle.

sessions.desc: As generative AI moves from conversation to execution, your agents need reliable access to enterprise capabilities. sessions.t: Untrusted code, unprecedented speed: High-velocity runtimes for AI agents
sessions.desc: The rise of autonomous AI agents presents a dual challenge: scaling agent throughput and safely running untrusted, LLM-generated code. This session will explore techniques for building agent infrastructure that address both performance and security while bringing Gemini’s power to AI code-execution use cases. We’ll demonstrate how to enable subsecond cold starts for massive concurrency, and how gVisor’s kernel-level isolation provides a zero-trust environment to protect your infrastructure. We’ll also examine how LangChain, a leading AI agent platform, is leveraging this architecture to deliver autonomous agent experiences at massive scale.

sessions.t: Use Model Context Protocol (MCP) Tools with ADK Agents
sessions.desc: Unify your AI integrations with the Model Context Protocol (MCP). MCP standardizes how LLMs access external data and tools. In this advanced workshop, you will explore two critical workflows: configuring an Agent Development Kit (ADK) agent to consume resources from existing MCP servers, and building a custom MCP server to expose your own ADK tools to outside clients. Learn to create seamless, bi-directional connections between your agents and external applications using this powerful open standard.

sessions.t: User-journey-first observability using SLOs, Gemini, and Cloud Monitoring
sessions.desc:
When your alerts lack context, you lose sight of the user experience. But when relevant context is spread across many sources of record, pulling it all together into a coherent story becomes a challenge.
See how The Home Depot solves this problem with their open source-compatible observability platform based on Cloud Monitoring. Their SLO alerts trigger Gemini to correlate data across disparate data sources and suggest a course of action, tying everything back to the SLO to highlight the impact of outages from a user experience perspective.
sessions.q:

sessions.t: What's in the box?
sessions.desc: Explore what’s really happening inside AI and LLM systems, and why it matters amid rising costs. As teams move faster with managed models and agent frameworks, spend is driven by token usage, latency, downstream services, and Google Cloud utilization. In this talk, you’ll encounter a practical, cost-first approach to running AI platforms by treating AI tooling like a junior engineer. You’ll also learn how observing LLM workflows and correlating model signals with application telemetry and cloud spend help teams spot inefficiencies, improve user experience, and keep AI innovation from becoming a cost impediment using Datadog. By attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow-up for this event only.

sessions.t: Why AI fails inside organizations (and how to fix It)
sessions.desc:
When AI stalls inside organizations, it's rarely because the models aren't powerful enough. In this session, Dr. Rebecca Hinds, Head of the Work AI Institute at Glean, identifies five critical ways organizations either set AI up to drive real results, or set it up to fail. She shows how decisions about roles, rewards, and workflows influence whether capable AI systems transform how people work—and why overlooking these organizational fundamentals is what holds many AI initiatives back.





By attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow up for this event only.
sessions.q:
why ai fails inside organizations (and how to fix it) when ai stalls inside organizations, it's rarely because the models aren't powerful enough. in this session, dr. rebecca hinds, head of the work ai institute at glean, identifies five critical ways organizations either set ai up to drive real results, or set it up to fail. she shows how decisions about roles, rewards, and workflows influence whether capable ai systems transform how people work—and why overlooking these organizational fundamentals is what holds many ai initiatives back.

sessions.t: Your data, your models, your future: The new standard for Confidential AI
sessions.desc: Security should never be a bottleneck to innovation. Join us to see how Google Cloud is future-proofing privacy for the AI era. We’ll unveil a comprehensive set of innovations ranging from post-quantum cryptography key management to the expansion of Confidential AI systems across CPUs and GPUs on Google Cloud. Learn how these capabilities allow you to process sensitive data and train proprietary models with the highest level of assurance, protecting your assets against both current and future threats.
sessions.q: your data, your models, your future: the new standard for confidential ai security should never be a bottleneck to innovation. join us to see how google cloud is future-proofing privacy for the ai era. we’ll unveil a comprehensive set of innovations ranging from post-quantum cryptography key management to the expansion of confidential ai systems across cpus and gpus on google cloud. learn how these capabilities allow you to process sensitive data and train proprietary models with the highest level of assurance, protecting your assets against both current and future threats. anand pashupathy intel amit patil google cloud nelly porter google cloud

sessions.t: Your data, your rules: The verifiable and private future powered with TEEs
sessions.desc: Unlock the full potential of sensitive AI workloads without compromising privacy. Trusted execution environments (TEEs) have emerged as a critical technology to address data privacy and collaboration concerns. Learn how to process your most sensitive data within hardware-isolated enclaves, ensuring your AI innovations and datasets remain encrypted in use and exclusively yours. Gain insights from customers on how they are leveraging Confidential Space for new business cases – from enabling a new generation of data clean rooms to helping users verify their identities with online services while keeping underlying information private.
sessions.q: your data, your rules: the verifiable and private future powered with tees unlock the full potential of sensitive ai workloads without compromising privacy. trusted execution environments (tees) have emerged as a critical technology to address data privacy and collaboration concerns. learn how to process your most sensitive data within hardware-isolated enclaves, ensuring your ai innovations and datasets remain encrypted in use and exclusively yours. gain insights from customers on how they are leveraging confidential space for new business cases – from enabling a new generation of data clean rooms to helping users verify their identities with online services while keeping underlying information private. rémi colin self rene kolga google cloud valerie mercurio infosum keith moyer google cloud

sessions.t: Zero to scale: Rapid development with Google Cloud & MCP Toolbox for database
sessions.desc: This session tackles AI's biggest production problem — stale, disconnected data. We explore how the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Google's open-source MCP Toolbox give agents real-time, grounded access to AlloyDB, BigQuery, and Spanner through simple YAML tool definitions. See two live demos: Prism, a multi-database retail agent that routes natural language to the right database; and a DB Builder agent that designs and extends full database backends from a plain English description.
sessions.q: zero to scale: rapid development with google cloud & mcp toolbox for database this session tackles ai's biggest production problem — stale, disconnected data. we explore how the model context protocol (mcp) and google's open-source mcp toolbox give agents real-time, grounded access to alloydb, bigquery, and spanner through simple yaml tool definitions. see two live demos: prism, a multi-database retail agent that routes natural language to the right database; and a db builder agent that designs and extends full database backends from a plain english description. mahesh kumar goyal google

sessions.t: Zero-friction data science with BigQuery and Vertex AI
sessions.desc: Join the group to discuss accelerating data science with BigQuery and Vertex AI. We'll explore how to empower analysts with SQL-based predictions and data scientists with Spark/Python in a unified, governed environment. The group will examine BigQuery Studio and AI-first Colab notebooks, utilizing built-in agents to streamline workflows. We’ll discuss bridging the gap to action by converting insights into interactive data applications.
sessions.q: zero-friction data science with bigquery and vertex ai join the group to discuss accelerating data science with bigquery and vertex ai. we'll explore how to empower analysts with sql-based predictions and data scientists with spark/python in a unified, governed environment. the group will examine bigquery studio and ai-first colab notebooks, utilizing built-in agents to streamline workflows. we’ll discuss bridging the gap to action by converting insights into interactive data applications. ellery berk google cloud

sessions.t: Zero-friction data science: From raw data to inferencing in the fast lane
sessions.desc: Supercharge your data science workflows with BigQuery and Vertex AI. Join this session to explore the unified, governed environment where analysts gain SQL-based predictive capabilities and data scientists get the flexibility of Apache Spark and Python for rich experimentation, model management, and operations. Experience BigQuery Studio and AI-first Colab notebooks, featuring a built-in agent to plan and execute your workflows. And learn how to bridge the gap between analysis and action by transforming insights into interactive data applications.
sessions.q: zero-friction data science: from raw data to inferencing in the fast lane supercharge your data science workflows with bigquery and vertex ai. join this session to explore the unified, governed environment where analysts gain sql-based predictive capabilities and data scientists get the flexibility of apache spark and python for rich experimentation, model management, and operations. experience bigquery studio and ai-first colab notebooks, featuring a built-in agent to plan and execute your workflows. and learn how to bridge the gap between analysis and action by transforming insights into interactive data applications. seamus abshere faraday ellery berk google cloud mahit murthy google cloud

sessions.t: Zero-ops GKE: How Palo Alto Networks composes secure, scalable systems
sessions.desc: Scaling distributed systems requires shifting from manual management to automated orchestration – a transition Palo Alto Networks has mastered. Join this session to go inside the architectural choices powering their high-performance environments. We’ll explore Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) Autopilot for a near-zero-ops experience and the technical implementation of horizontal pod autoscaling (HPA) to handle traffic without over-provisioning. We’ll also pull back the curtain on how they used Workload Identity Federation to replace static secrets. Leave with a real-world blueprint for composing scalable, secure, and lean systems.
sessions.q: zero-ops gke: how palo alto networks composes secure, scalable systems scaling distributed systems requires shifting from manual management to automated orchestration – a transition palo alto networks has mastered. join this session to go inside the architectural choices powering their high-performance environments. we’ll explore google kubernetes engine (gke) autopilot for a near-zero-ops experience and the technical implementation of horizontal pod autoscaling (hpa) to handle traffic without over-provisioning. we’ll also pull back the curtain on how they used workload identity federation to replace static secrets. leave with a real-world blueprint for composing scalable, secure, and lean systems. mingxiao guo palo alto networks gari singh google cloud
